reptext: Chest PA and lateral radiograph. The lungs are clear bilaterally with no consolidation or pleural effusion. The cardiac silhouette is normal in size. No pneumothorax identified. Bony structures appear intact.

CT chest with contrast. A 1.2 cm pulmonary nodule in the right upper lobe may represent a primary lung lesion. Mediastinal lymph nodes appear mildly enlarged. Follow-up CT in three months is recommended.
Portable chest radiograph. Bilateral perihilar infiltrates consistent with pulmonary oedema. The heart appears moderately enlarged. Small bilateral pleural effusions are likely present.

High-resolution CT chest. Bilateral ground-glass opacities in the lower lobes suggesting interstitial lung disease. No pleural effusion. Findings are consistent with a possible inflammatory or fibrotic process.
Chest radiograph post cardiac catheterisation. The lungs remain clear. Cardiac silhouette is enlarged but stable compared to prior imaging. No new consolidation or effusion identified.

CT abdomen and pelvis without contrast. The liver, spleen, and pancreas appear unremarkable. Mild thickening of the gallbladder wall may indicate acute cholecystitis. No free intraperitoneal air identified.

Ultrasound of the abdomen. The liver demonstrates increased echogenicity consistent with hepatic steatosis. The gallbladder contains multiple small calculi. Both kidneys appear normal in size and echotexture.

CT abdomen with contrast. A 3 cm hypodense lesion in the right hepatic lobe possibly represents a simple cyst, though metastatic disease cannot be excluded. Clinical correlation and further imaging advised.

Abdominal radiograph erect and supine. Multiple air-fluid levels in the small bowel could suggest early mechanical obstruction. No evidence of free air under the diaphragm. Urgent clinical review is advised.

MRI brain without contrast. No acute intracranial abnormality identified. The ventricles and sulci are appropriate for age. There is mild periventricular white matter change consistent with small vessel disease.
CT head without contrast. No intracranial haemorrhage, infarct, or mass effect detected. Grey-white matter differentiation appears preserved. The bony calvarium is intact.

MRI brain with and without contrast. A 2 cm ring-enhancing lesion in the left frontal lobe likely represents a high-grade glioma, though metastasis cannot be excluded. Urgent neurosurgical review is recommended.

MRI lumbar spine without contrast. Moderate disc degeneration at L4-L5 and L5-S1. A posterior disc protrusion at L4-L5 appears to compress the traversing left L5 nerve root. No cord signal abnormality.

Cervical spine radiograph AP and lateral. Degenerative changes at C5-C6 with mild disc space narrowing. No acute fracture or subluxation identified. Straightening of the cervical lordosis may suggest paraspinal muscle spasm.

CT lumbar spine without contrast. Severe degenerative disc disease from L3 to S1. Significant spinal canal stenosis at L4-L5 could account for the reported neurogenic claudication. Bone density appears reduced.

MRI right knee without contrast. Full-thickness tear of the anterior cruciate ligament is identified. A horizontal cleavage tear of the medial meniscus at the posterior horn is present. Moderate joint effusion noted.

Radiograph of the right knee AP and lateral. Moderate medial compartment joint space narrowing consistent with osteoarthritis. No acute fracture identified. Mild osteophyte formation at the medial tibial plateau.
MRI pelvis with contrast. Bulky fibroid uterus with multiple intramural fibroids, the largest measuring 5 cm. The ovaries appear normal bilaterally. No free pelvic fluid identified.
CT pelvis with contrast. The prostate gland appears mildly enlarged at approximately 45 cc, consistent with benign prostatic hyperplasia. No suspicious pelvic lymphadenopathy. Bony pelvis is intact.

MRI right shoulder without contrast. Full-thickness tear of the supraspinatus tendon measuring approximately 2 cm in the anteroposterior dimension. Moderate glenohumeral joint effusion present. Mild fatty infiltration of the rotator cuff musculature.
